ingredients.prep: 14-15 oz (or use another can of diced)

steps.text: Prep fish: Cut fish into large chunks. Pat dry and season lightly with a pinch of salt and black pepper. Keep chilled.
Sweat the trinity: In a Dutch oven over medium heat, warm oil and optional butter. Add onion, bell pepper, and celery. Cook until softened. Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds.
Bloom tomato paste: Stir in tomato paste and cook 1 minute to deepen flavor.

Build the stew base: Add diced tomatoes, crushed tomatoes, stock, bay leaves, thyme, oregano, paprika, cayenne, Creole seasoning, Worcestershire, optional hot sauce, remaining black pepper, and part of the salt. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Simmer and reduce: Simmer until the sauce thickens slightly and tastes well-developed. If it gets too thick, add a splash of stock.
Poach fish gently: Reduce heat to low. Nestle fish chunks into the simmering sauce. Cover and cook just until fish is opaque and flakes easily. Avoid boiling so the fish stays intact.
Finish: Remove bay leaves. Stir in lemon juice, most green onions, and most parsley. Taste and adjust salt, pepper, and heat.
Serve: Spoon courtbouillon over hot cooked rice. Garnish with remaining green onions and parsley.
